content_en:
> Uthman bin ‘Affi: Dow an-Nurayn ‘Uthmin ibn ‘Affin ibn Abii al-As ibn Umayyah al-Qurashi al-Amawi, the third Rightly Guided Khaljfah and one of the ten promised Paradise. He was known for his generosity and freely giving in the Way of Allah, He was married to two daughters of the Prophet (#2), Rugayyah and after her death, Umm Kulthiim. He was martyred in the year 35H.

* ‘All bin Abi ‘Talib: ibn ‘Abdul-Muttalib ibn Hashim al-Qurashi al-Hashimi, the fourth Rightly Guided Khaljfzh and one of ten promised Paradise. He accepted Islam at the age of thirteen and was famous for his chivalry, bravery and knowledge. He married Fatimah, the daughter of the Prophet (#8) and was martyred in the year 40H.

5 Abii ad-Dard@’: Uwaymie ibn Malik ibn Zayd ibn Qays al-Khazraji al-Ansari. There is a difference of opinion concerning his name. He accepted Islam on the day of Badrand witnessed Ubud. He was from the Legal Jurists and ascetics of the Companions. He died in the year 32H.

ing the year of Khaybar and reported some ahddith from the Prophet (4g). He died in the year 52H.

SEEDS OF ADMONISHMENT AND REFORM

Devotees ate focused upon worshipping their Lord; their feet ate lined up in harmony within the rows [of Sa/dh], standing [in Salah\ their weight on the throes of fear [of Allah] so when they ate affected by their fear [of Allah] they shift their weight off the foot of fear to the foot of hope.

The condition of people differ at the time of their death; some endured the fear [of Allah] until the moment they died, such as ‘Umar Ibn al-Khattab (radiyAllahu ‘anhn), who said on his death bed: ‘woe to ‘Umar should his Lord not forgive him’, and some were distressed by the thirst of their fearful cautiousness so they cooled themselves down with the water of hope, such as Bilal ibn Abi Rabah! (radiyAllahu ‘anhu), whose wife lamented while he was on his death bed saying; "how grievous and sorrowful is this moment for me!’ while he on the other hand said jubilantly ‘how joyous and pleasant a moment this is, soon I will meet Muhammad and his companions!’ This is because Bilal (radiyAllahu ‘anbu) knew that the Imam who lead the Sa/dh [i.e. the Prophet (:g)| would never forget the one who announced the call for Sa/dh [i.e. Bilal] and so he mitigated death with the comfort of hope.

While Sulayman al-Taimi* was on his death bed he said to his son: ‘Narrate to me the hadiths related to concessions, so that I meet Allah, Exalted is He, while I am optimistic in Him.’ For how

7 Bilal ibn Abi Rabah: His story is almost certainly the most famous of all of the Companions of the Prophet (#8). He was born in Makkah into slavery, and was tortured by his Umayya ibn Khalaf when he refused to renounce his new faith. He was purchased by Abii Bakr as-Siddiq, who set him free. He became the Prophet’s muezzin at Medinah, and later moved to Syria, where he died in the year 17H

In the Saying of Allab: “The reckoning of mankind draws ever near”

long should a traveler walk. Surely he must stop and seek repose at some point.

O you of feeble will: How far are you from the path on which Adam (‘alayhi as-salam) suffered, because of which Ni (‘a/ayhi assalam) mourned, for the sake of which Ibrahim (‘a/ayhi as-salami) was thrown into fire, on which Ishaq (‘a/ayhi as-salam) was laid down to be slaughtered, Yusuf (‘alayhi as-salam) was sold for a meager price, Zakariyyah (‘a/ayhi as-salam) was cut into pieces, Yahya (‘alayhi as-salam) was murdered, Ayyab (‘alayhi as-salam) suffered all kinds of calamities, Dawad (‘a/ayhi as-salim) wept so much, Sulayman (‘alayhi as-salam) did not rejoice despite all that he possessed, Missa (‘alayhi as-salam) desited to see His Lord, ‘Isa (‘alayhi as-salam) wandered with the beasts in the desert, and Muhammad (48) endured poverty!

The Marvelous Creation of Allah

adorn the brides of creation, to delight the eyes of the

slaves [of Allah]; such wonders! From the moment you are admitted into the kingdom [of Allah] you shall never stop admiring the adornments [of His creation] so, look for a moment with the eyes of contemplation while lowering the gaze of your bodily eyes, and then answer me; is there any better universe than this? Leave out of your house of retreat, and walk across the passage of your contemplation and stop to contemplate at the wide open spaces; reflect on how the green grows in the presence of the Creator’s mysteries; growing plants all wearing the same garb of solidity; dancing in the springtime in celebration of the season of irrigation they had. Contemplate the multitude of colours that exist in a single branch, how splendid is the handiwork of the Creator!

( OLOURFUL ARE THE JEWELS of existence that

Pay attention to their signs, and notice how they all direct the heedless to the One Who created them, While people are busy in their self-desires. Listen to the sound of leaves while they are hanging on their sticks so may the rhyme of their movements bring

The Marvelous Creation of Allah

back the one who is far [to the right path].

I wonder how you reflect on others yet forget reflecting on yourself! You should use the eye of your intellect to reflect deeply on how a drop of seed is dipped in the blood of menses is being crafted by the carpenter of fate shaping the faculties of hearing and seeing without even touching it, all the while it is embraced while wearing its garment and covering up with a robe of a clot to transform afterward into a lump of flesh. Then it changes to flesh and blood so afterward is covered with skin to protect it from the hands of harm until eventually coming out in the form of infant who gradually grows up to become a child; then after some time he experiences the sphere of utterance and climbs out the robe of intellect and reasoning. There are many calls that have been voiced out between the legs of transforming due to the shaking shackles of wisdom in the bangles of mind. Every time it makes a sound the tongues of guidance chant songs of meanings but how would a reckless deaf hear all that? Far from happening it is, for a person who suffers the flu cannot enjoy the smell of toses similar to how a petson in prison cannot enjoy the day of ‘Jd. So, if you were aware of all these wonders yet have not been amazed then be surprised of how you have not so far!!

How could you not be thoughtful though you attained the instruments of intellect and reasoning! How could you blind your heart after seeing the truth?! Indeed, the most amazing acts of Allah’s Power is when

“Allah has sent him astray due to knowledge.” {al-Jathiyah 45; 23]

Mankind is in Need of Allah, The Most High

eternal. As the child is born He sparks for him a river of

milk coming out from the springs of his mother’s breast and implanted instinctual love towards him into the hearts of his parents until He overwhelms them with His grace. Yet, after this child grows up and becomes aware of the One Who bestowed all these graces upon him, he utilises all these graces in disobeying Allah, As the poet have said:

T HE LENIENCY OF ALLAH toward His slaves is pre-

A person spend his nights wearing the robe of impiety, Nevertheless Allah covers him with the shelter of His forbearance without knowing so.

After he reaches the age of puberty and realises the peak of his strength he rushes to issue forth a challenge [i-e. start involving in acts of disobedience]! But for whose interest? And against whom? To get to where? And who are you in the first place to proclaim a challenge? May Allah annihilate the one who does not appreciate His favours upon him.

Mankind is in Need of Allah, The Most High

O youl Allah has bestowed upon you a mercy incomparable to any other mercy; when you repent from your sins He orders his Angels to overlook what he recorded against you, and when He judges you He covers your shortcomings so that others do not see the yellowness of your skin caused by your shame. O you whose innate nature is that of purity, beware of debasing yourself with the filth of sins. Lift up the tails of piety above the trash of self-desires and beware of the sprays of mistakes so it does not touch your pure garments.

‘To observe your duties you should firmly shield yourself [from sins] so look ahead because

feck, pe Me inalts zbec gee) site evita “But if you sip [i-e., deviate] after clear _ have come to you, then know that Allah is Exalted in Might and Wise.”

And know that the fountain of your eyes shall wash off the ditt staining your heart.

Since your affaits were sound at yout first day of

Berge escet 94 hese he

Pathe an bos pSle pote SaT iy

“And [mention] when your Lord took from the children of Adam - from their loins - theit descendants and made them testify of themselves, [saying to them], ‘Am I not your Lord?”

SEEDS OF ADMONISHMENT AND REFORM

Thus you better rectify your affairs at the end so that what is in between be forgiven. O you who has been fat away from his home whete it was said

thee Bore “Am I not your Lord”

did you not long for it yet? As the poet said:

Give your heart to whoever you desire .... for Love is only granted to the first lover

O you who turned away from his Lord, what else kept you away from your Lord and so you turned to instead? Why have you let yourself be led by another? [It is time] for you to stand on the right path.
content_id:
> Utsman bin 'Affan: Dzu an-Nurayn, 'Utsman ibn 'Affan ibn Abi al-'Ash ibn Umayyah al-Qurasyi al-Umawi, Khalifah Rasyidin ketiga dan salah satu dari sepuluh orang yang dijanjikan masuk Surga. Ia dikenal karena kedermawanan dan keikhlasannya dalam berinfak di Jalan Allah. Ia menikahi dua putri Nabi (#2), Ruqayyah dan setelah wafatnya, Umm Kultsum. Ia wafat sebagai syahid pada tahun 35H.

* 'Ali bin Abi Thalib: ibn 'Abdul-Muttalib ibn Hasyim al-Qurasyi al-Hasyimi, Khalifah Rasyidin keempat dan salah satu dari sepuluh orang yang dijanjikan masuk Surga. Ia masuk Islam pada usia tiga belas tahun dan terkenal karena kesatriaan, keberanian, dan keilmuannya. Ia menikahi Fatimah, putri Nabi (#8) dan wafat sebagai syahid pada tahun 40H.

5 Abu ad-Darda': Uwaymir ibn Malik ibn Zayd ibn Qays al-Khazraji al-Anshari. Terdapat perbedaan pendapat mengenai namanya. Ia masuk Islam pada Perang Badar dan menyaksikan Perang Uhud. Ia termasuk dalam kalangan fuqaha dan ahli zuhud dari para Sahabat. Ia wafat pada tahun 32H.

pada tahun Khaybar dan meriwayatkan beberapa hadith dari Nabi (4g). Ia wafat pada tahun 52H.

BENIH-BENIH NASIHAT DAN PEMBARUAN

Para ahli ibadah berfokus pada penyembahan kepada Rabb mereka; kaki-kaki mereka berjejer selaras dalam barisan [salah], berdiri [dalam salah] dengan bertumpu pada beban rasa takut [kepada Allah], sehingga ketika mereka dipengaruhi oleh rasa takut [kepada Allah] mereka memindahkan tumpuan dari kaki rasa takut ke kaki rasa harap.

Keadaan manusia berbeda-beda di saat ajal menjemput mereka; sebagian menanggung rasa takut [kepada Allah] hingga saat mereka wafat, seperti 'Umar ibn al-Khaththab (radiyAllahu 'anhu), yang berkata di ranjang kematiannya: 'celakalah 'Umar jika Rabbnya tidak mengampuninya', dan sebagian lainnya dilanda dahaga akibat kewaspadaan yang penuh rasa takut sehingga mereka mendinginkan diri dengan air harapan, seperti Bilal ibn Abi Rabah (radiyAllahu 'anhu), yang istrinya meratap saat ia berada di ranjang kematiannya dengan berkata: "betapa sedih dan dukanya saat ini bagiku!" sementara ia sendiri berkata dengan gembira: "betapa menyenangkan dan bahagianya saat ini, sebentar lagi aku akan berjumpa dengan Muhammad dan para sahabatnya!" Hal ini karena Bilal (radiyAllahu 'anhu) mengetahui bahwa Imam yang memimpin salah [yakni Nabi (:g)] tidak akan pernah melupakan orang yang mengumandangkan adzan untuk salah [yakni Bilal] dan ia pun meredakan kematian dengan ketenangan harapan.

Ketika Sulayman al-Taimi* berada di ranjang kematiannya, ia berkata kepada putranya: 'Sampaikanlah kepadaku hadith-hadith yang berkaitan dengan keringanan-keringanan, agar aku bertemu Allah, Yang Maha Mulia, dalam keadaan bersangka baik kepada-Nya.' Karena betapa

7 Bilal ibn Abi Rabah: Kisahnya hampir pasti merupakan yang paling masyhur di antara seluruh Sahabat Nabi (#8). Ia lahir di Makkah dalam kondisi perbudakan, dan disiksa oleh tuannya Umayyah ibn Khalaf ketika ia menolak meninggalkan imannya yang baru. Ia dibeli oleh Abu Bakr ash-Shiddiq, yang kemudian memerdekakannya. Ia menjadi muadzin Nabi di Madinah, dan kemudian pindah ke Syam, di mana ia wafat pada tahun 17H.

Dalam Firman Allah: "Telah dekat kepada manusia hari perhitungan mereka"

lama seorang musafir harus berjalan. Sungguh ia pasti harus berhenti dan mencari istirahat pada suatu saat.

Wahai engkau yang lemah kemauan: Betapa jauhnya engkau dari jalan yang padanya Adam ('alayhi as-salam) menderita, yang karenanya Nuh ('alayhi as-salam) berduka, yang demi-Nya Ibrahim ('alayhi as-salam) dilemparkan ke dalam api, yang di atasnya Ishaq ('alayhi as-salam) dibaringkan untuk disembelih, yang karenanya Yusuf ('alayhi as-salam) dijual dengan harga yang murah, yang padanya Zakariyyah ('alayhi as-salam) dipotong-potong, yang karenanya Yahya ('alayhi as-salam) dibunuh, yang padanya Ayyub ('alayhi as-salam) menanggung berbagai macam musibah, yang karenanya Dawud ('alayhi as-salam) banyak menangis, yang padanya Sulayman ('alayhi as-salam) tidak bergembira meski dengan segala yang ia miliki, yang padanya Musa ('alayhi as-salam) berhasrat untuk melihat Rabbnya, yang padanya 'Isa ('alayhi as-salam) mengembara bersama binatang-binatang di padang pasir, dan yang padanya Muhammad (48) menanggung kemiskinan!

Keajaiban Ciptaan Allah

menghiasi pengantin-pengantin ciptaan, untuk menyenangkan mata para

hamba [Allah]; sungguh menakjubkan! Sejak saat engkau diizinkan masuk ke dalam kerajaan [Allah] engkau tidak akan pernah berhenti mengagumi keindahan [ciptaan-Nya], maka lihatlah sejenak dengan mata perenungan seraya menundukkan pandangan matamu yang lahiriah, lalu jawablah aku; adakah alam semesta yang lebih indah dari ini? Keluarlah dari rumah khalwatmu, dan berjalanlah melintasi lorong perenunganmu dan berhentilah untuk merenung di ruang-ruang terbuka yang luas; renungkanlah bagaimana hijau tumbuh di hadapan misteri-misteri Sang Pencipta; tumbuhan-tumbuhan yang semuanya mengenakan pakaian yang sama berupa kokohnya; menari-nari di musim semi dalam perayaan musim penyiraman yang telah mereka lalui. Renungkanlah banyaknya warna yang ada dalam satu cabang, betapa indahnya karya tangan Sang Pencipta!

PENUH WARNA ADALAH PERMATA-PERMATA keberadaan yang

Perhatikanlah tanda-tanda mereka, dan perhatikan bagaimana semuanya mengarahkan orang-orang yang lalai kepada Dia Yang menciptakan mereka, sementara manusia sibuk dengan hawa nafsu mereka sendiri. Dengarkanlah suara dedaunan saat mereka bergantung pada ranting-rantingnya agar irama gerakannya dapat membawa kembali

Keajaiban Ciptaan Allah

orang yang telah jauh [ke jalan yang benar].

Aku heran bagaimana engkau merenungkan orang lain namun melupakan perenungan terhadap dirimu sendiri! Hendaknya engkau menggunakan mata akalmu untuk merenungkan secara mendalam bagaimana setetes benih yang dibenamkan dalam darah haid sedang dibentuk oleh tukang kayu takdir yang membentuk indera pendengaran dan penglihatan tanpa menyentuhnya sama sekali, sementara semuanya itu terkandung seraya mengenakan pakaiannya dan berselimutkan jubah segumpal darah untuk kemudian berubah menjadi segumpal daging. Lalu berubah menjadi daging dan darah sehingga kemudian dibalut dengan kulit untuk melindunginya dari tangan bahaya hingga akhirnya keluar dalam wujud bayi yang secara bertahap tumbuh menjadi seorang anak; lalu setelah beberapa waktu ia memasuki ranah ucapan dan memanjat keluar dari jubah akal dan penalaran. Terdapat banyak seruan yang telah dikumandangkan di antara tahap-tahap perubahan akibat guncangan belenggu kebijaksanaan pada gelang-gelang pikiran. Setiap kali terdengar suara, lidah-lidah hidayah menyanyikan lagu-lagu makna, namun bagaimana mungkin orang yang tuli dan lalai dapat mendengar semuanya itu? Jauh kemungkinannya, karena seseorang yang menderita flu tidak dapat menikmati harumnya mawar, sama seperti seseorang yang berada dalam penjara tidak dapat menikmati hari 'Id. Maka, jika engkau mengetahui semua keajaiban ini namun belum juga merasa takjub, maka heranlah atas dirimu yang sejauh ini belum juga merasa takjub!!

Bagaimana mungkin engkau tidak berpikir padahal engkau telah memperoleh perangkat akal dan penalaran! Bagaimana mungkin engkau membutakan hatimu setelah melihat kebenaran?! Sesungguhnya, perbuatan Kekuasaan Allah yang paling menakjubkan adalah ketika

"Allah telah menyesatkannya berdasarkan ilmunya." [al-Jatsiyah 45: 23]

Manusia Membutuhkan Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi

abadi. Ketika seorang anak dilahirkan Dia mengalirkan baginya sungai

susu yang keluar dari mata air dada ibunya dan menanamkan rasa cinta yang bersifat naluriah kepadanya ke dalam hati kedua orang tuanya hingga Dia melimpahi mereka dengan karunia-Nya. Namun, setelah anak ini tumbuh dewasa dan menyadari Siapa yang telah menganugerahkan semua karunia ini kepadanya, ia menggunakan seluruh karunia tersebut untuk bermaksiat kepada Allah. Sebagaimana yang telah diucapkan oleh sang penyair:

KELEMBUTAN ALLAH terhadap para hamba-Nya adalah pra-

Seseorang menghabiskan malamnya berselimutkan jubah kefasikan, Namun Allah tetap menutupinya dengan naungan kesabaran-Nya tanpa ia menyadarinya.

Setelah ia mencapai usia balig dan menyadari puncak kekuatannya, ia bergegas untuk melontarkan tantangan [yakni mulai terlibat dalam perbuatan-perbuatan maksiat]! Namun demi kepentingan siapa? Dan kepada siapa? Untuk menuju ke mana? Dan siapakah engkau sejatinya sehingga berani menyatakan tantangan? Semoga Allah membinasakan orang yang tidak mensyukuri karunia-Nya kepadanya.

Manusia Membutuhkan Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi

Wahai engkau! Allah telah menganugerahkan kepadamu rahmat yang tiada bandingnya dengan rahmat manapun; ketika engkau bertaubat dari dosa-dosamu Dia memerintahkan para Malaikat-Nya untuk mengabaikan apa yang telah dicatat terhadapmu, dan ketika Dia mengadilimu Dia menutupi kekuranganmu agar orang lain tidak melihat kekuningan kulitmu akibat rasa malumu. Wahai engkau yang fitrahnya adalah kesucian, berhati-hatilah agar engkau tidak merendahkan dirimu dengan kotoran dosa. Angkatlah ujung jubah ketakwaan di atas tumpukan hawa nafsu dan berhati-hatilah terhadap percikan-percikan kesalahan agar tidak menodai pakaianmu yang suci.

'Untuk menunaikan kewajiban-kewajibanmu hendaknya engkau melindungi dirimu dengan kokoh [dari dosa-dosa], maka pandanglah ke depan karena

feck, pe Me inalts zbec gee) site evita "Namun jika kamu tergelincir [yakni menyimpang] setelah bukti-bukti nyata datang kepadamu, maka ketahuilah bahwa Allah Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Bijaksana."

Dan ketahuilah bahwa air mata dari matamu akan membasuh kotoran yang mencemari hatimu.

Karena urusan-urusanmu baik pada hari pertamamu

Berge escet 94 hese he

Pathe an bos pSle pote SaT iy

"Dan [ingatlah] ketika Tuhanmu mengambil dari keturunan anak Adam - dari tulang punggung mereka - keturunan mereka dan menjadikan mereka bersaksi atas diri mereka sendiri, [dengan berfirman kepada mereka], 'Bukankah Aku Tuhanmu?'"

SEEDS OF ADMONISHMENT AND REFORM

Maka perbaikilah urusanmu di penghujung agar apa yang ada di antaranya diampuni. Wahai engkau yang telah jauh dari rumahmu di mana dikatakan

thee Bore "Bukankah Aku Tuhanmu?"

apakah engkau belum merindukannya? Sebagaimana kata sang penyair:

Serahkan hatimu kepada siapa pun yang engkau kehendaki .... karena Cinta hanya dianugerahkan kepada kekasih pertama

Wahai engkau yang berpaling dari Tuhannya, apa lagi yang membuatmu jauh dari Tuhanmu sehingga engkau berpaling kepada selain-Nya? Mengapa engkau membiarkan dirimu dipimpin oleh yang lain? [Sudah saatnya] bagimu untuk berdiri di jalan yang benar.
content_ar: > عثمان bin ‘Affi: Dow an-Nurayn ‘Uthmin ibn ‘Affin ibn Abii al-As ibn Umayyah al-Qurashi al-Amawi, the ثالثاً Rightly Guided Khaljfah and one of the ten promised الجنة. He was known for his الكرم and freely giving in the الطريق of الله, He was married to two daughters of النبي ﷺ (#2), Rugayyah and بعد her الموت, Umm Kulthiim. He was martyred in the year 35H. * ‘All bin Abi ‘Talib: ibn ‘Abdul-Muttalib ibn Hashim al-Qurashi al-Hashimi, the رابعاً Rightly Guided Khaljfzh and one of ten promised الجنة. He accepted الإسلام at the age of thirteen and was famous for his chivalry, bravery and العلم. He married Fatimah, the daughter of النبي ﷺ (#8) and was martyred in the year 40H. 5 Abii ad-Dard@’: Uwaymie ibn مالك ibn Zayd ibn Qays al-Khazraji al-Ansari. There is a difference of opinion فيما يتعلق بـ his name. He accepted الإسلام on the day of Badrand witnessed Ubud. He was from the Legal Jurists and ascetics of the الصحابة. He died in the year 32H. ing the year of Khaybar and reported some ahddith from النبي ﷺ (4g). He died in the year 52H. SEEDS OF ADMONISHMENT AND REFORM Devotees ate focused upon worshipping their Lord; their feet ate lined up in harmony within the rows [of Sa/dh], standing [in الصلاة\ their weight on the throes of الخوف [of الله] لذا عندما they ate affected by their الخوف [of الله] they shift their weight off the foot of الخوف to the foot of الأمل. The condition of people differ at the time of their الموت; some endured the الخوف [of الله] حتى the moment they died, مثل ‘عمر Ibn al-Khattab (radiyAllahu ‘anhn), who said on his الموت bed: ‘woe to ‘عمر should his Lord not forgive him’, and some were distressed by the thirst of their fearful cautiousness لذا they cooled themselves down with the water of الأمل, مثل Bilal ibn Abi Rabah! (radiyAllahu ‘anhu), whose الزوجة lamented بينما he was on his الموت bed saying; "how grievous and sorrowful is this moment for me!’ بينما he من ناحية أخرى said jubilantly ‘how joyous and pleasant a moment this is, soon I will meet محمد ﷺ and his الصحابة!’ This is لأن Bilal (radiyAllahu ‘anbu) knew that the الإمام who lead the Sa/dh [i.e. النبي ﷺ (:g)| would never forget the one who announced the call for Sa/dh [i.e. Bilal] and لذا he mitigated الموت with the comfort of الأمل. بينما سليمان ﷺ al-Taimi* was on his الموت bed قال to his son: ‘Narrate to me the hadiths related to concessions, لذا that I meet الله, Exalted is He, بينما I am optimistic in Him.’ For how 7 Bilal ibn Abi Rabah: His story is almost certainly the most famous of all of the الصحابة of النبي ﷺ (#8). He was born in مكة المكرمة into slavery, and was tortured by his Umayya ibn Khalaf عندما he refused to renounce his new الإيمان. He was purchased by Abii Bakr as-Siddiq, who set him free. He became النبي ﷺ’s muezzin at Medinah, and later moved to Syria, where he died in the year 17H In the Saying of Allab: “The reckoning of manki
